title: California Investing $202 Million to Expand Cleaner Transportation Options in Communities Most Affected by Pollution
content: Caltrans announced the award of $202 million to 143 local clean transportation projects aimed at prioritizing public transit and electric vehicle options in California communities most affected by pollution. Funded through California Climate Investments under the Low Carbon Transit Operations Program, the initiative builds on more than $1.4 billion invested over the past decade to reduce carbon emissions. The funding supports expanded bus and rail services, zero emission vehicle purchases, zero emission infrastructure, and free or reduced fare programs to encourage fewer and shorter automobile trips. According to Caltrans, the investments will improve public health, strengthen mobility access, and help advance the stateâ€™s climate goals, particularly in disadvantaged communities. The program directs Cap and Invest dollars toward reducing greenhouse gases from major emission sources while driving innovation and supporting a clean energy economy. Since its launch, LCTOP has funded 1,548 projects statewide, benefiting transit agencies and riders through improved service, cleaner fleets, and more affordable transportation options that better serve communities impacted by air pollution.
author: Analysts Account
